meta.description: Network and HTTP packages for Standard Library 2.0

objective: Implement panther.net and panther.http packages wrapping existing network and HTTP primitives

panther_implemented_logic.description: All 55 functions implemented in .pan files under stdlib/panther/net/__init__.pan and stdlib/panther/http/__init__.pan
panther_implemented_logic.pattern: Self-hosted PantherLang wrappers that delegate to Python-backed stdlib primitives

host_abi_primitives.description: No new Host ABI primitives required. All package functions delegate to existing Python-backed stdlib functions which use existing Host ABI backends where available (native_time, native_filesystem, native_crypto, native_socket).

native_backends.description: No new native backends. Existing backends (libc, libcrypto) used transitively via existing stdlib functions.

python_fallbacks.description: All 55 functions ultimately delegate to Python-backed stdlib functions in compiler/stdlib/functions.py. These functions have native fallbacks where applicable (time, sleep, read_file, write_file, file_exists, mkdir, sha256, tcp_connect, tcp_banner).

full_regression.note: All existing test suites pass without regression

example_results.description: Package functions verified working in PantherLang programs
example_results.sample: panther main { print(panther_net_local_ip()); print(panther_net_network_class("10.0.0.1")); let r = panther_http_get("https://api.example.com"); print(r); }

known_limitations: HTTP headers not supported: Current http_request doesn't support custom headers. panther_http_with_headers is a placeholder.
No TLS/SSL configuration: HTTP client uses system defaults.
No cookie/session management: Each request is independent.
No async HTTP: All operations are blocking.
TCP server is oneshot by default: net_tcp_serve_start creates a one-shot server unless oneshot=false.
IP classification Linux-specific: Uses /proc and ip command fallbacks.
next_phase: Phase 5 — Web Foundation (panther.web) with HTTP server, routing, middleware, static files, error handling
